Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Boomloom: Think with your hands 95 upvotes, 8 comments
  2. Sandboxing: Foolproof Boundaries vs. Unbounded Foolishness (2025) 19 upvotes, 0 comments
  3. The paddle wheel aircraft carriers of Lake Michigan 74 upvotes, 8 comments
  4. Show HN: Termcraft – terminal-first 2D sandbox survival in Rust 108 upvotes, 19 comments
  5. Show HN: Atomic – Self-hosted, semantically-connected personal knowledge base 89 upvotes, 15 comments
  6. Electronics for Kids, 2nd Edition 157 upvotes, 32 comments
  7. A digital resource for studying the graffiti of Herculaneum and Pompeii 18 upvotes, 1 comments
  8. ZJIT removes redundant object loads and stores 83 upvotes, 16 comments
  9. Common Lisp Development Tooling 79 upvotes, 16 comments
  10. Floci – A free, open-source local AWS emulator 150 upvotes, 38 comments
  11. Some things just take time 626 upvotes, 196 comments
  12. Professional video editing, right in the browser with WebGPU and WASM 221 upvotes, 63 comments
  13. Bayesian statistics for confused data scientists 95 upvotes, 25 comments
  14. Sashiko: An agentic Linux kernel code review system 14 upvotes, 1 comments
  15. Grafeo – A fast, lean, embeddable graph database built in Rust 212 upvotes, 69 comments
  16. Hide macOS Tahoe's Menu Icons 174 upvotes, 60 comments
  17. Trivy ecosystem supply chain briefly compromised 57 upvotes, 20 comments
  18. Cloudflare flags archive.today as "C&C/Botnet"; no longer resolves via 1.1.1.2 105 upvotes, 43 comments
  19. Do Not Turn Child Protection into Internet Access Control 629 upvotes, 336 comments
  20. The three pillars of JavaScript bloat 161 upvotes, 77 comments
  21. My first patch to the Linux kernel 6 upvotes, 0 comments
  22. It's Their Mona Lisa 15 upvotes, 3 comments
  23. Tinybox – A powerful computer for deep learning 445 upvotes, 264 comments
  24. Alpha Micro AM-1000E and AM-1200 11 upvotes, 2 comments
  25. Thinking Fast, Slow, and Artificial: How AI Is Reshaping Human Reasoning 124 upvotes, 69 comments
  26. How Ford burned $12B in Brazil (2021) 49 upvotes, 23 comments
  27. Chest Fridge (2009) 77 upvotes, 51 comments
  28. How Invisalign became the biggest user of 3D printers 155 upvotes, 115 comments
  29. Ubuntu 26.04 Ends 46 Years of Silent sudo Passwords 358 upvotes, 350 comments
  30. Training Center for Maneuvering on Manned Model Ships 7 upvotes, 5 comments
